The '''''Dargon''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] that served the Empire as a prison ship.

==History==
Its escort typically included [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avengers]] and [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]].

Carrying [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] prisoners, it came under attack from Rebel forces bent on rescuing prisoners, at least twice.

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the player (acting as [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]) prevents the ''Dargon'' from being disabled, ensuring that the prisoners can be brought to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Lord Vader]] and interrogated by him. The Rebels sent three waves of starfighters then two [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] called [[Keeper (corvette group)|''Keeper'']] and an [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] called ''[[Hut'to]]''. In another mission involving the ''Dargon'', its engines shut down, possibly through sabotage. The Rebels then attack again with a [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] called ''[[Bay Point]]'' with [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] and [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]].

In ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'', the Rebels [[Rescue from the Dargon|attempt]] to disable the ''Dargon'' with starfighters and the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80]] cruiser ''[[Columbia]]''. The outcome of the event involving the ''Dargon'' depends on which side the player takes.
